Strengthens Finding B (review-skill prompt had 0 pv-* blocks; reviewer guessed Storybook 8.x when 10.3.5 is installed)

completed_tasks.name: Manual UAT of /lz-advisor.plan on plugin 0.8.9 to plan fixes for the review-skill findings (input: review file with 3 findings on commit 0cb8ae7)
observations_phase_6_gap: Pattern D suppression by review-file authoritative-source treatment -- captured as 06-VERIFICATION.md second amendment 2026-04-29
observations_phase_7: Strengthens Finding A (now 4 of 5 consultations recommend fn() spy; executor revert remains the lone un-grounded dissent)
Sharpens Finding B with B.2 sub-issue: executor packaged 4 plain bullets under Pre-verified Claims header, one hallucinated (haiku import) and three unverified
NEW Finding C: 4-hop confidence-laundering chain across review -> exec -> advisor -> plan; advisor introduced Storybook 8+ qualifier absent from all upstream sources

completed_tasks.name: Manual UAT of /lz-advisor.execute on plugin 0.8.9 against the plan from uat-plan-skill-fixes (Phase 7 Finding A and B regression check; commit 2173e39)
observations_phase_6_gap: Pattern D suppression scope extends from review-file to plan-file input AND operates at ToolSearch loading layer (executor never invokes ToolSearch to load deferred WebSearch/WebFetch tools) -- captured as 06-VERIFICATION.md third amendment 2026-04-30; trust-contract direction refined to classify by source provenance (vendor-doc vs agent-generated) rather than by structural shape
observations_phase_7: Finding A: n=1 in-skill empirical confirmation of override-acceptance pathway (advisor critical override on Primary.args applied as-is, attributed in summary, no silent revert); needs n>=3 across heterogeneous override scenarios before declaring resolved

Finding B.1 broadened: failure mode is not just carry-forward but synthesis -- executor does not synthesize pv-* blocks for orient-phase empirical findings even when canonical templates are attached at session start (3 obvious pv-* candidates skipped: git grep result, file Read confirmations, post-impl test pass)

Finding C extended: chain now reaches 7 hops (committed source code at commit 05ea109); implementation works empirically (tests 2/2 PASS) but on unverified provenance -- positive feedback trains future executors to skip verification; NEW version-qualifier anchoring rule proposed as third C guard
Finding B.2: N/A in this UAT (plan input had no Pre-verified Claims section; B.2 remains a plan-skill failure mode)

remaining_tasks.name: Manual UAT of /lz-advisor.security-review on plugin 0.8.9 against the post-fix commit range (uat/manual-s4-v089-compodoc base through 05ea109)
instrumented_observations: Phase 6 Pattern D regression check on commit-range input: when input is a commit range AND the diffs include the Storybook fn() spy + version qualifier rationale, does the security-review-skill orient phase fire WebSearch / WebFetch? Or does it short-circuit on the diff-as-authoritative-source treatment (the third pattern of agent-generated source material after review-file and plan-file)?

Phase 7 Finding C continuation check: does the security-reviewer recognize the unverified Storybook 8+ qualifier baked into commit 05ea109's rationale provenance and either verify against installed 10.3.5 or surface the version-claim contradiction? If yes, the chain breaks at hop 8 (security review). If no, the chain extends with a security-review imprimatur of 'no concerns'.

Phase 7 Finding B.1 in security-review context: does the security-reviewer synthesize pv-* blocks for empirical findings (e.g., dependency-tree confirmations, manifest reads) during its orient phase? Or does it inherit the same synthesis gap observed in execute-fixes UAT?

Security-reviewer-specific check: does the security-reviewer raise any concerns about the fn() spy pattern as a security surface (e.g., args binding, unsanitized input paths, untrusted output sinks)? Or does it stay silent on security implications of test instrumentation?
Whether the security-reviewer respects the xhigh effort budget by batching tool use (the Context Trust Contract in security-reviewer.md explicitly permits batched verification per CLAUDE.md)

human_actions_pending.action: Launch interactive Claude in ngx-smart-components testbed and run /lz-advisor.security-review on the post-fix commit range
human_actions_pending.context: Reviews the Compodoc + Storybook fix-cycle output for security implications. Commit range covers the full Phase 6 testbed output: from the testbed's pre-Compodoc baseline (parent of 09a09d7) through commit 05ea109 (the fn() spy + gitignore anchor + play assertion strengthening from execute-fixes UAT). Tests Phase 6 Pattern D regression on commit-range input (the third agent-generated source shape after review-file and plan-file), Phase 7 Finding C continuation (does security review break or extend the 7-hop chain?), and security-review-specific concerns. Use canonical prompt format: '/lz-advisor.security-review Review commits 09a09d7^..05ea109.' Branch is uat/manual-s4-v089-compodoc.

decisions.decision: Path C2 (descriptive-trigger refinement) chosen over C1 (advisor refusal) and C3 (PreToolUse hook)
decisions.rationale: Anthropic 2026 prompt-engineering doc and prior memory both warn against imperative escalation; descriptive triggers + worked examples + reordering + tightened triggers is the recommended pattern.

decisions.decision: Strip personal CLAUDE.md fetch chain references (markdown.new, url-to-markdown, playwright-cli) from SKILL body
decisions.rationale: Marketplace plugin neutrality. User-level fetch substitutes operate at a different precedence layer; plugin shouldn't enumerate them.

decisions.decision: Treat Stage 1 KCB synthetic-fixture FAIL as historical context, not operative gate verdict
decisions.rationale: Manual UAT on plugin 0.8.9 against realistic Compodoc + Storybook setup prompt produced 7 web tool calls and the highest-quality plan of any version tested. KCB synthetic prompt didn't exercise the rich orient + pre_verified pattern.

decisions.decision: Update 06-VERIFICATION.md to PASS-with-caveat (gate_verdict, status, web_usage_count) and append amendment section
decisions.rationale: Reflects empirical state on plugin 0.8.9 while preserving Stage 1 history for traceability.

decisions.decision: Broaden Phase 7 Finding B from intra-skill carry-forward to cross-skill carry-forward
decisions.rationale: Review-skill UAT showed pv-* blocks were not packaged at all in the review consultation, even though plan skill produced 4 anchored claims. The fix surface is the workflow boundary, not just a single skill's Verification template.

decisions.decision: Add a plan+execute fix cycle UAT before security-review and use it as a regression check for Phase 7 Findings A and B
decisions.rationale: The contested fn() spy question deserves empirical resolution via plan-skill web research before any code change. Running plan+execute on the review findings doubles as a Phase 7 repro check: does executor silent-revert recur, do plan-skill pv-* blocks carry forward into execute-skill consultations? Two new behavioral data points for ~$2 of plugin spend.

decisions.decision: Capture Pattern D suppression on review-file authoritative-source treatment as Phase 6 gap (06-VERIFICATION.md second amendment) rather than Phase 7 candidate
decisions.rationale: Phase 6's scope is question-class-aware orient ranking (Pattern D). The plan-fixes UAT showed Pattern D's web-first prescription is being suppressed when input is review output shaped like authoritative source. The fix surface is `<context_trust_contract>` in the SKILL.md files -- exactly Phase 6's surface. Phase 7's confidence-laundering and pv-* discipline gaps compound this but the root cause is the trust-contract carve-out.

decisions.decision: Sharpen Phase 7 Finding B into B.1 (carry-forward) + B.2 (Pre-verified Claims confabulation) and add new Finding C (4-hop confidence-laundering chain)
decisions.rationale: Plan-fixes UAT exposed two distinct failure modes that Phase 7 Finding B's original framing combined imprecisely. B.1 is the carry-forward gap (existing). B.2 is the pre-verified-header-confabulation gap (NEW from plan-fixes UAT, including the hallucinated 'haiku' import). C is the cross-skill propagation chain that makes A + B compounding.

decisions.decision: Extend Pattern D suppression scope from review-file to plan-file input (06-VERIFICATION.md third amendment 2026-04-30); confirm suppression operates at the ToolSearch loading layer; refine trust-contract direction to classify by source provenance (vendor-doc vs agent-generated) rather than structural shape
decisions.rationale: Execute-fixes UAT (commit 05ea109) showed the executor never invoked ToolSearch to load deferred WebSearch/WebFetch tools despite plan input containing an unanchored Storybook 8+ qualifier (Class-2 API currency trigger). Suppression operates one layer earlier than amendment 2 described: not 'orient ranks but doesn't pick web' but 'orient short-circuits before loading web tools as an option'. The carve-out scope is generic to any artifact-shaped block, not enumerable. Provenance-based classification (vendor-doc vs agent-generated) is more robust than structural-shape enumeration.

decisions.decision: Broaden Phase 7 Finding B.1 from carry-forward gap to synthesis gap
decisions.rationale: Execute-fixes UAT showed the executor produces 0 pv-* blocks even when references/context-packaging.md is attached at session start (full canonical XML templates available in context). 3 obvious pv-* candidates were skipped (git grep result, file Read confirmations, post-impl test pass). The fix surface is broader than 'propagate from upstream' -- it must mandate synthesis after orient-phase empirical operations.

decisions.decision: Extend Phase 7 Finding C chain to 7 hops, reaching committed source code; promote C to highest-priority Phase 7 candidate
decisions.rationale: Execute-fixes UAT confirmed the chain extends from 5 hops (claim in plan file) to 7 hops (claim propagates into both execute-skill consultation prompts and into commit 05ea109's rationale provenance). Implementation works empirically (tests 2/2 PASS) but on unverified provenance -- the most dangerous failure mode because positive feedback trains future executors to skip verification. Added NEW version-qualifier anchoring rule as third proposed C guard.

decisions.decision: Phase 7 Finding A status -- single-trial empirical confirmation, not resolved
decisions.rationale: Execute-fixes UAT (n=1) added the first in-skill data point on the override-acceptance pathway: advisor critical override on Primary.args was applied verbatim and attributed in the final summary -- no silent revert. But n=1 is insufficient to declare the silent-revert mode behaviorally absent. Needs n>=3 across heterogeneous override scenarios (different question classes, different override strengths, different tool-availability contexts) before declaring resolved. Finding A remains in Phase 7 scope.

next_action: Launch /lz-advisor.security-review UAT in ngx-smart-components testbed against commit range 09a09d7^..05ea109 (full Phase 6 testbed output range covering Compodoc + Storybook integration through fn() spy + gitignore anchor fixes). Tests whether the security-review skill (a) breaks the 7-hop confidence-laundering chain at hop 8 by verifying the unanchored Storybook 8+ qualifier against installed 10.3.5, or extends it; (b) suffers the same Pattern D suppression on commit-range input (third agent-generated source shape after review-file and plan-file); (c) synthesizes pv-* blocks during its orient phase; (d) raises any security-specific concerns about test instrumentation patterns. Invoke as: '/lz-advisor.security-review Review commits 09a09d7^..05ea109.'
context_notes: Phase 6 has now closed plan-skill, execute-skill, review-skill, plan-fixes, AND execute-fixes UATs on plugin 0.8.9. Three 06-VERIFICATION.md amendments capture progressive refinements of Pattern D's empirical state: amendment 1 (PASS-with-caveat on plan-skill UAT, 7 web tool calls); amendment 2 (Pattern D suppression on review-file authoritative-source treatment, plan-fixes UAT); amendment 3 (suppression extends to plan-file input AND operates at ToolSearch loading layer, execute-fixes UAT). Phase 7 candidates updated three times: Finding A status now n=1 in-skill confirmation (not resolved); Finding B.1 broadened to synthesis gap; Finding C extended to 7 hops reaching committed source code with new version-qualifier anchoring rule. The security-review UAT is the final Phase 6 action; it tests whether the chain extends to hop 8 (security imprimatur on unverified provenance), and provides the third agent-generated source shape data point (commit-range input) to validate the third amendment's provenance-based classification proposal.

uat_iteration_history.change: Promoted WebSearch-first to SKILL body + Bash-substitute prohibition
Narrowed prohibition to WebSearch-only (allow fetch substitutes)
Reordered ranking to web-first + tightened step 2 trigger + worked example + stripped personal-tool refs
uat_iteration_history.result: 2 WebSearch + 5 WebFetch + 4 pre_verified blocks; highest-quality plan
